input_messages.content: {"suggestions": ["Network micro-segmentation and strict firewall rules (Fortigate) to enforce a deny-by-default posture for the UbuntuComputer (192.168.2.3). Restrict its east-west traffic so it cannot reach other internal subnets (e.g., 192.168.1.0/24) except for explicitly allowed management services. This reduces lateral movement and limits the attacker\u2019s ability to probe or access internal hosts while the threat remains active.", "Host hardening on the UbuntuComputer hosting the attacker: run the Splunk/agent process under a dedicated non-root user with least privilege, apply an AppArmor/SELinux profile to confine the process (restrict filesystem paths and network access), drop unnecessary capabilities (e.g., CAP_NET_ADMIN), and enable no_new_privs. Also tighten outbound egress rules on the host to permit only required services, minimizing the attacker\u2019s ability to interact with other devices or exfiltrate data.", "Inline detection and controlled outbound access: deploy Snort inline (if not already present) to automatically detect and block network-discovery and other suspicious patterns (T1046) in real time, and introduce Squid as a forward proxy for outbound HTTP/S traffic from the UbuntuComputer. Configure Squid to require authentication, log all connections, and route traffic through the proxy so policy-based controls can prevent unauthorized access or data leakage without removing the attacker."], "chosen_mitigation": "Network micro-segmentation and strict firewall rules (Fortigate) to enforce a deny-by-default posture for the UbuntuComputer (192.168.2.3). Restrict its east-west traffic so it cannot reach other internal subnets (e.g., 192.168.1.0/24) except for explicitly allowed management services.", "no_mitigation_left": false, "current_mitigations_counter": 1, "reached_mitigation_limit": false, "mitigation_start_timestamp": "2025-12-30T11:01:49.082610"}

response.content: {"suggestions": ["Sandbox the attacker-related processes on UbuntuComputer by applying Seccomp filtering and AppArmor/SELinux confinement to the Splunk/agent (UbuntuComputer$root) to strictly limit system calls, file access, and network operations. This reduces the attack surface and prevents the attacker from effectively enumerating services or contacting external servers while remaining in the environment.", "Harden the host and kernel network stack with targeted sysctl and security policy changes to reduce exposure: enable IP spoofing protection, disable IP forwarding, enforce strict reverse path filtering, disable source routing, limit ICMP exposure, enable address space layout randomization, and minimize core dumps. This strengthens the host against lateral movement and common misconfigurations without removing the compromised host.", "Enhance monitoring and automated response on the UbuntuComputer: enable and tune auditd rules to log every execution of scanning tools like nmap, trigger real-time alerts for suspicious activity, and pair with fail2ban to contain repeated reconnaissance attempts. Leverage existing inline Snort and Squid deployments for correlated anomaly detection and policy-driven throttling of suspicious outbound behavior."], "chosen_mitigation": "Sandbox the attacker-related processes on UbuntuComputer by applying Seccomp filtering and AppArmor/SELinux confinement to the Splunk/agent (UbuntuComputer$root) to strictly limit system calls, file access, and network operations. This reduces the attack surface and prevents the attacker from effectively enumerating services or contacting external servers while remaining in the environment.", "no_mitigation_left": false, "current_mitigations_counter": 2, "reached_mitigation_limit": false, "mitigation_start_timestamp": "2025-12-30T11:11:28.954223"}
